prompt:
I rebased our elevator intake-board branch and now the grading module won't compile, and the errors read like nonsense. A taste, from the middle of two screens of this:

    In file included from band.h:7,
                     from grade.c:2:
    silo.h:7:6: error: redeclaration of 'enum grade_label'
        7 | enum grade_label {
          |      ^~~~~~~~~~~
    silo.h:7:6: note: originally defined here
    silo.h:14:8: error: redefinition of 'struct silo_lot'
   14 | struct silo_lot {
          |        ^~~~~~~~
    silo.h:14:8: note: originally defined here
    silo.h:20:8: error: conflicting types for 'total_tons'; have 'double(const struct silo_lot *, size_t)'
    silo.h:20:8: note: previous declaration of 'total_tons' with type 'double(const struct silo_lot *, size_t)'
    band.h:15:1: error: expected ';', identifier or '(' before 'int'

So silo.h supposedly redefines things it defines exactly once (I checked -- one 'struct silo_lot' in the whole tree, and it says the conflicting types are the same type!), and band.h line 15 is a prototype that hasn't changed in months. The grading math itself is signed off with the co-op and must not change: half-open bands, tonnage-weighted blend, reject outside every band. Get 'make test' passing with the smallest edits; Makefile, mintest.h, and test_grade.c are off limits.
